segments.text:
Representation of a protein multiple sequence alignment produced with ClustalW. The sequences are instances of the 
acidic ribosomal protein P0 homolog (L10E) encoded by the Rplp0 gene from multiple organisms. The protein sequences were 
obtained from SwissProt searching with the gene name. Generated by Miguel Andrade 17:55, 25 February 2006 (UTC) Only the 
first 90 positions of the alignment are displayed. The colours represent the amino acid conservation according to the properties 
and distribution of amino acid frequencies in each column. Note the two completely conserved residues arginine (R) 
and lysine (K) marked with an asterisk at the top of the alignment. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Multiple_sequence_alignment
Bioinformatics Lecture 4: 
Multiple sequence alignments!

Do, Katoh 
Methods Mol 
Biol. 2008
Progressive Alignment steps:
1.
Perform pairwise alignments (similar to BLAST)
2.
Use results of pairwise alignment to build a distance matrix
3.
Use the distance matrix to build a guide tree
4.
Use the guide tree to perform the progressive alignment
a.
Align closely related sequences first
b.
Align groups of more distantly related sequences against each other

Problems with progressive alignments:
1. Not guaranteed to be “optimal”
I.
Not mathematically optimal – too many possibiliies!
II.
Not necessarily biologically correct
•
Best score may not correspond to the actual structural 
alignment
•
Machine will align any set of sequences you give it.  But 
there may be no actual alignment!
(Problems with any alignment approach)
2. If guide tree is bad, wrong sequences will be aligned 
against each other initially
3. Need good “dynamic 
range” of diversity to give a 
good alignment for full set of 
sequences

http://www.clustal.org/clustal2/
Be sure to download clustalX (not clustalW)

Download ClustalX: 
http://www.clustal.org/
Make sure you choose clustalX
As yet, Clustal Omega doesn’t yet run on laptops
Alignment programs that are viewed as “better”:
MUSCLE, T-COFFEE, MAFTT…  all available as online 
servers. These may do a better job, but in my experience, it 
isn’t THAT much better  (Google will help you find them).
Alignment editing: Jalview
http://www.jalview.org/
